steps.description: Type text 'copilot connection app' in the search bar located at the top of the 'Samples' tab within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface. And input ${{var:app_name:copilot-connection-app}} into the run time variable.

Click the "Create" button for the "Copilot connect..." sample in the "Samples" panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the blue button at the red crosshair position.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the default folder path in the "Workspace Folder" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion the page title contains 'Copilot connector app'
Right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code editor to open its context menu.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu of the tab header to close the currently active tab in Visual Studio Code.
Click the “+"  browser tab in Microsoft Teams  to open the new page
Type the URL 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the browser's address bar at the top of the Google Chrome window.
Press the 'Enter' key to navigate to the URL "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab" entered in Chrome's address bar.
Click the "Advanced" button on the browser's "Your connection is not private" error page at coordinates (256, 511).
Click on the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link in the browser's "Your connection is not private" warning page.
Click the browser tab titled "(1) graph-connector-applocal | teams.microsoft.com" at coordinates (141, 19) to switch context.
Click the "Turn on" button in the notification bar at the top of the Microsoft Teams web interface to enable desktop notifications.
Click the "Reload app" option in the dropdown menu within the Microsoft Teams web application interface.
Click the "Allow" button in the popup requesting network device connection access for teams.microsoft.com.
Click the scroll bar to scroll down the page
Click the "Ingest Data" button in the "2. Ingest Sample Data into Copilot connector" section to begin processing sample data using the Azure Functions app.
@assertion the sysytem returns the 401 error indicating missing admin consents for the required permissions.
